LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT OFFICER NEFF, OFFICER KOENIG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE ADMINISTRATIVE CLAIMS REQUIREMENT BARS ANY RELIEF AGAINST THE DEMURRING DEFENDANTS. PLAINTIFF DID NOT NOTIFY OPPOSING COUNSEL OF HIS INTENT TO APPEAR AND ARGUE. AS A RESULT THE COURT COULD NOT HEAR ARGUMENT AND ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF INGRAM, KENNETH JEROME TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT NEFF, OFFICER ERIC BADGE 696 KOENIG, OFFICER RAYMOND SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
THERE WAS NO APPEARANCE, NOR OBJECTION FILED. CASE DISMISSED AS TO DEFENDANT JANE DOE, TRAFFIC COURT COMMISSIONER BY COURT AT ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE HEARING DEC-07-2009 AT 1:30PM. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kenneth Jerome Ingram, 510-832-5015, plaintiff and opposing party appearing propria persona

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT OFFICER NEFF, OFFICER KOENIG'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT, Sustained without leave to amend. The failure to comply with the administrative claims requirement bars any relief against the demurring defendants. PLAINTIFF DID NOT NOTIFY OPPOSING COUNSEL OF HIS INTENT TO APPEAR AND ARGUE. AS A RESULT THE COURT COULD NOT HEAR ARGUMENT AND ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077(301/HEK)
